example_prompt: Add Djomy payment link to my payment workflow.

countryCode.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code in uppercase (e.g. GN for Guinea).

amountToPay.description: Fixed payment amount. If omitted, the payer enters the amount themselves on the payment page.

linkName.description: Name for the link, useful to identify it in the merchant dashboard.

description.description: Context for the payment, shown in the dashboard.

phoneNumber.description: Payer's phone number. Required if sendSms is true.

sendSms.description: If true, Djomy sends the payment link to phoneNumber via SMS. Requires phoneNumber to be set. Defaults to false.

usageType.description: UNIQUE: the link deactivates after one successful payment. MULTIPLE: the link can be used for multiple payments.

usageLimit.description: Maximum number of payments allowed through this link (for MULTIPLE usage type).

expiresAt.description: ISO 8601 datetime after which the link is automatically deactivated.

merchantReference.description: Your own reference for this payment link.

returnUrl.description: URL to redirect the payer to after payment.

cancelUrl.description: URL to redirect the payer to if they cancel.

allowedPaymentMethods.description: Payment methods to display on the payment page. Defaults to all available methods.

customFields.description: Custom form fields to add to the payment page.

metadata.description: Custom key-value data echoed back in webhooks. Must be flat JSON (no arrays, no nested objects).

response_schema.description: All responses are wrapped in { success, message, data, error, errors, timestamp, status }.

paymentLinkReference.description: Use this to retrieve the link later. The field 'reference' is deprecated — use paymentLinkReference.

status.description: ACTIVE = ready to receive payments. REVOKED = manually deactivated. For UNIQUE links, status becomes REVOKED after one successful payment.

paymentPageUrl.description: Public URL of the payment page. Share this with the payer.

payments.description: List of successful payments made through this link. Always empty in the creation response for UNIQUE links — use verify_payment to check status after payment.

gotchas: Use paymentLinkReference (not reference) to retrieve or track the link. The reference field is deprecated according to the API spec.
For UNIQUE links, Djomy automatically deactivates the link after the first successful payment. Do not rely on a second payment going through.
sendSms: true requires phoneNumber to be set. If phoneNumber is omitted, the SMS will not be sent and no error is returned.
Auth requires two steps: (1) compute HMAC-SHA256(clientId, clientSecret) as hex, (2) call POST /v1/auth with header X-API-KEY: clientId:hexSignature to get a Bearer token.
countryCode must be uppercase (GN, SL, LR). The API validates against the pattern ^[A-Z]{2}$ and will reject lowercase values.
